sections.paragraph:
Θεσσαλονίκη, ης, ἡ (Polyb. 22, 11, 2; Strabo 7 Fgm. 24; Ps.-Lucian, Asin. 46; anon. De Historia Diadochorum [time unknown]: 155 Fgm. 2, 3 Jac.; ins [SIG index vol. IV 94]) Thessalonica, a city in Macedonia on the Thermaic Gulf. Paul founded a congregation here Ac 17:1, 11, 13; Phil 4:16; 2 Ti 4:10.—BHHW III 1968; DACL XV 1 and 624–713; Kl. Pauly V 761–63.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

anon. anon. = anonymous

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

vol. vol. = volume

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DACL DACL = Dictionnaire d’Archéologie chrétienne et de Liturgie—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 454
